_comment: CODEX — optional Suzerain-style lore briefs (never required to read). Reachable from the slide-out menu. No preset-person names; the player's line is referenced via tokens, not literals.

entries.title: The Convergence (1800s)
entries.body: The first century of the line is a CONVERGENCE: a hundred waves of hungry people pour into one young country and learn, against each other and beside each other, what it costs to belong. Your line arrives with nothing the new world is obliged to honour. What it builds in these decades — a foothold, a trade, a name spoken without apology — is the floor every later generation stands on. The era forgives nothing and forbids nothing.

entries.title: The Emergence (1900s)
entries.body: By the second century the immigrant becomes the established. EMERGENCE is the long climb out of arrival: into comfort, into the institutions, into a name that carries past the ward. A line that meant to stay poor — austere, rebellious, devoted — holds its rung on purpose; every other line reaches for the next. The century's great turns — its booms, its wars, its reinventions — lift the line that leans with them and grind the one that leans against.

entries.title: The Ascension (2000s and beyond)
entries.body: The third movement is ASCENSION: the line stops being shaped by the world and begins to shape it — the nation, then the globe, then the dark beyond the sky. The question is no longer whether the family survives but what mark it leaves on the cosmos, and whether it reaches the stars alone, alongside the lines it once crossed, or not at all.

entries.title: The Irish Wave
entries.body: Driven out by a hunger that emptied whole parishes, the Irish arrived poorest and most distrusted, and answered scorn with the two things they could not be denied: numbers and the ward. Faith and community were their capital; the parish in exile was a bank that paid in loyalty. A line from this wave climbs through who it knows before what it owns.

entries.title: The Bavarian Wave
entries.body: The German-speaking lines came with trades in their hands — brewers, bakers, machinists, players — and a habit of building the institution rather than storming it: the guild, the hall, the society. They prospered quietly and were trusted until the century's wars made their names suddenly foreign. A line from this wave builds standing brick by patient brick.

entries.title: The Italian Wave
entries.body: From the stony south they came late and in force, mistrusted as clannish and bound by an honour-code the new country mistook for menace. Family was first, last, and everything; the line that thrives keeps its own counsel and pays every debt of loyalty twice over. Honour and lineage are the axes a line of this wave lives and dies on.

entries.title: The Ashkenazi Wave
entries.body: Fleeing the pogroms of the Pale, these lines arrived with the portable wealth of the persecuted: literacy, a tradition of argument, and a long memory of how fast a foothold can be taken away. They moved into the trades the gentiles would not touch and the professions that could not be confiscated. A line of this wave hedges, learns, and never fully trusts the floor it stands on.

entries.title: The Scandinavian Wave
entries.body: From frozen fields with no land left to inherit, the Nordic lines chased the homestead dream onto the cold open middle of the continent. They were taken for plain, patient, and Protestant, and they were — a line of this wave keeps its head down, its word good, and its eye on the long horizon rather than the near reward.

entries.title: The Chinese Wave
entries.body: Across the widest ocean they came for Gold Mountain and the rails, and met a country that took their labour and then wrote laws to keep them out. Excluded from the ward and the ballot alike, the line that endures builds a city within the city and a patience that outlasts the men who legislated against it. Reach and endurance are its quiet weapons.

entries.title: The Baghdad Wave
entries.body: The oldest thread in the weave: a line whose memory runs back to a House of Wisdom and a caliphate's dawn, carrying scholarship and trade across a millennium before the modern crossing. Where other lines began in the 1800s, this one began far earlier — and remembers it. A line of this wave carries worldview and tradition like an inheritance older than the country it now enters.
